name: xf_cep_not_order_mixed_initial
comment: D-145 FENCED (order-only) mixed-regime `not E0() P()` witness — INITIAL P's on BOTH sides of the blocker ([P1, E0, P2]): the shape neither D-140 (blocker-first) nor D-143 (P-first, fuzz_notorder_b puts ALL initial P's before the blocker) ever generated. DETERMINISTIC: oracle [3,2,1] stable; engine (D-143 seg model, P-first latched via P1) emits [2,3,1] — it orders seg1's inserts purely by gidx, but the oracle demotes the POST-BLOCKER EPOCH-0 INITIAL (P2) to a last-class tail behind the epoch insert (P3). CANDIDATE RULE (hand-probed 10/10, $JOB/tmp/mprobe.py, NOT yet population-validated): segments newest-first as D-143; WITHIN a segment = [epoch>=1 inserts, gidx asc] ++ [updates, apply-seq desc] ++ [epoch-0 initials, gidx asc]. An epoch-0 initial UPDATED at all (even same-segment) promotes into the updates slot (m_updP2mid/unb); an epoch>=1 insert updated same-segment stays an insert (D-143 nb801x0). Reduces to D-143 exactly when no segment holds post-blocker initials, and explains m_2before [4,3,1,2] / m_2after [4,2,3,1] / m_upd2 [3,1,2]. FIRING SET 0-div; order-only; outside every fuzzer distribution + certified corpus (fence-lifted sweeps clean). To close: extend fuzz_notorder_b with mixed initial positions -> population 0-div -> extend seg_order_key with the initials-last class. See DECISIONS D-143/D-144/D-145; ~/.claude/plans/cep-item-1b-findings.md.
drl: rule NE when not E0() P() then end
